items.q: How do you say 'I love films' in Spanish?
items.why: Students translate 'amar' (which is for people/love) or forget gustar/encantar structure. ENCANTAR works like gustar - backwards. Subject = películas (plural), so verb = ENCANTAN. 'Me encanta el cine' (singular subject) vs 'me encantan las películas' (plural).

items.q: In Spain, how do you address a group of friends informally?
items.why: Students don't distinguish vosotros (informal plural, Spain) from ustedes (formal plural in Spain, but used for all plural in Latin America). In Spain: tú/vosotros = informal; usted/ustedes = formal. Latin America: only ustedes for plural.

items.correct: Could be his/her/your(formal)/their brother - context determines which.
items.why: Students assume su = his. SU is one of Spanish's most context-dependent words. Means his, her, your (usted/ustedes), or their. Speakers often clarify with 'de él', 'de ella', 'de usted' if ambiguous: 'el hermano de él'.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my sister'?
items.correct: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.misconception: Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.
items.random_wrong: Yo llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.why: Correct answer: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.. “Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is the correct use of ser/estar: 'I am tired'?
items.why: It's Estoy cansado.. “Soy cansado.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many forms does the Spanish adjective 'simpático' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 forms. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
